title: Green Romesco Prawns & Chickpeas
summary: Big, punchy flavours with minimum faff. This green romesco’s smoky-sweet vibes are perfect with juicy prawns & crispy chickpeas. A proper satisfying bowl.

method.description: Grill the green peppers until charred all over. Transfer to a bowl, cover and leave for 10 mins. Once cooled, peel off the blackened skin, remove the cores and seeds, then set aside.

Roughly tear two-thirds of the coriander. Remove the stem and seeds from the green chilli. Add the grilled peppers to a blender along with the torn coriander, the green chilli, most of the pumpkin seeds (save some for garnish) and ¾ of the sherry vinegar. Blitz until smooth, then season well with salt and black pepper.
Pick the remaining coriander leaves and roughly chop most of them, keeping a few whole for garnish. Peel and thinly slice the garlic.
Heat the olive oil in a nonstick frying pan set over a medium-high heat. Season the prawns with salt and black pepper, then fry for around 3 mins until pink and cooked through.
Add the garlic and chickpeas to the pan and cook for another minute, tossing everything together. Drizzle in the remaining sherry vinegar, stir through the chopped coriander, and season to taste.
To serve, spoon the romesco into bowls. Top with the prawns and chickpeas, then scatter over the reserved coriander leaves and pumpkin seeds.

chefs.name: Anna Helm Baxter
chefs.summary: Anna Helm Baxter is a cookbook author, food stylist, and food producer at Mob. Having spent most of her life in the US, she loves potlucks, tailgates, and a good Thanksgiving spread. As a working mum of three, she gets the dinner struggle, which is why she’s all about recipes that are joyful, healthy, and totally doable. And if all else fails she always has cookies stashed in the freezer.
